title: tithymaloides macrocarpa
intro: Tithymaloides macrocarpa, also known as the Titan Arum or Corpse Lily, is a rare and endangered flowering plant species native to Sumatra, Indonesia. The plant is known for its unique, strong odor resembling rotting flesh, which attracts carrion-feeding flies to facilitate pollination. The Titan Arum has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations involving Tithymaloides macrocarpa include infusions and decoctions, although its use and availability are largely limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
